name: Apple Foundation Model 3 Core Advanced (on-device, 20B sparse)
archDisplay: Sparse on-device model (20B total, 1-4B active per request) using Apple's 'Instruction-Following Pruning' with shared + input-routed experts stored in flash and loaded into DRAM on demand
note: Apple's most capable on-device model as of mid-2026, the headline of the WWDC26 third-generation Foundation Models family; natively multimodal (image understanding, expressive voices, higher-accuracy dictation).
verificationNotes: Confirmed via Apple's own ML research blog post plus independent coverage (9to5Mac, Memeburn, MacRumors, AppleInsider) of the June 8, 2026 WWDC26 keynote/Platforms State of the Union. ctxK=32 carried forward from the prior generation as an estimate (no new figure published in sources found). As with the sibling Core model, only internal human-preference win-rates were published (e.g. >61% preferred on image understanding vs the prior generation) rather than fixed-benchmark scores, so 'results' is left empty rather than guessing a mapping.
